channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: New species of glass frog identified in Ecuador
item.description: A new type of glass frog has been discovered in Ecuador, and researchers have named it after weightlifter Neisi Dajomes, the first Ecuadorian woman to win an Olympic gold medal.
item.content:encoded: A new type of glass frog has been discovered in Ecuador, and researchers have named it after weightlifter Neisi Dajomes, the first Ecuadorian woman to win an Olympic gold medal.
item.dc:creator: Gurjit Kaur

item.title: Israel is building a buffer zone inside Lebanon
item.description: Israel is creating a large buffer zone in southern Lebanon for a prolonged military occupation, with low expectations that direct talks with Lebanon will lead to quick action on disarming Hezbollah.
item.content:encoded: Israel is creating a large buffer zone in southern Lebanon for a prolonged military occupation, with low expectations that direct talks with Lebanon will lead to quick action on disarming Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Justice Department says Biden DOJ weaponized law to go after anti-abortion activists
item.description: The Trump administration has said that enforcement of the FACE Act by the Biden DOJ represents "the prototypical example" of the weaponization of the law against conservatives.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has said that enforcement of the FACE Act by the Biden DOJ represents "the prototypical example" of the weaponization of the law against conservatives.

(Image credit: Amanda Andrade-Rhoades)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Law enforcement is trying to combat abusive AI. Experts say easier said than done
item.description: An Ohio man was convicted of cybercrimes involving obscene AI-generated images of women and children. But experts warn of the difficulties in going after such cases.
item.content:encoded:
An Ohio man was convicted of cybercrimes involving obscene AI-generated images of women and children. But experts warn of the difficulties in going after such cases.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: The Iran war created a global natural gas shortage — a windfall for U.S. companies
item.description: With Qatar's liquefied natural gas still offline, U.S. companies see an opening and are bringing in new investments.
item.content:encoded:
With Qatar's liquefied natural gas still offline, U.S. companies see an opening and are bringing in new investments.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Historic decline in U.S. overdose deaths threatened by changing street drug supply
item.description: Drug overdose deaths are plummeting in the U.S. in ways never seen before. Experts worry new, toxic "synthetic" street drugs could derail the recovery.
item.content:encoded:
Drug overdose deaths are plummeting in the U.S. in ways never seen before. Experts worry new, toxic "synthetic" street drugs could derail the recovery.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein/AP)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Veteran diplomat offers insights into the war in Iran — and thoughts on what's next
item.description: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.
item.content:encoded: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: You can order your own blood work now. Interpreting the results is another story
item.description: Firms like Function Health and Oura market regular blood tests to people wanting to take their health into their own hands. The process often raises more questions for patients than it can answer.
item.content:encoded: Firms like Function Health and Oura market regular blood tests to people wanting to take their health into their own hands. The process often raises more questions for patients than it can answer.
item.dc:creator: Kate Cunningham

item.title: After losing loved ones, an Israeli and a Palestinian work together for Middle East peace
item.description: An Israeli whose parents were killed on Oct. 7, 2023, and a Palestinian whose brother died from injuries in Israeli custody say they've become like brothers. Their new book is The Future Is Peace: A Shared Journey Across the Holy Land.
item.content:encoded:
An Israeli whose parents were killed on Oct. 7, 2023, and a Palestinian whose brother died from injuries in Israeli custody say they've become like brothers. Their new book is The Future Is Peace: A Shared Journey Across the Holy Land.

(Image credit: Maya Levin for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
item.description: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.
item.content:encoded:
The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

(Image credit: Peter Morrison)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
